Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting significant renal dysfunction, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease with impaired concentrating ability. Blood glucose is normal but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and underlying etiology.
